opportunities.company_name: FOMC Rate Decision & Powell Press Conference
opportunities.recommended_action: reduce exposure pre-event, trade post-statement breakout
opportunities.rationale: FOMC holds rates at 3.5-3.75% as widely expected, but Powell faces a stagflation dilemma: WTI above $98 is driving forward inflation expectations higher while Feb payrolls printed a soft 92K. The dot plot and Powell's tone on oil-driven inflation vs a softening labour market is the critical driver for US indices, USD and gold. Reduce directional risk by 17 Mar close; best edge is a post-statement breakout or fade once the initial 20-30 point S&P spike/flush resolves.

February PPI prints 5.5 hours before the FOMC decision and is the last major upstream inflation read. Consensus is for ~+0.2% MoM. An upside surprise (+0.4%+) would reinforce higher-for-longer expectations and add to stagflation fears, pressuring indices and supporting USD; a soft print (<0.1%) gives Powell more flexibility and could trigger a brief risk-on relief bounce. Directional bias set at the print; no pre-positioning.

opportunities.event_name: European Central Bank Rate Decision & Lagarde Press Conference
opportunities.rationale: ECB meets the day after FOMC in an already dense central-bank week. EUR/USD already slipped to $1.14 on safe-haven USD demand from the Iran conflict. A surprise cut or markedly dovish Lagarde would push EUR/USD toward $1.10-$1.11 and relieve DAX; a hold with hawkish tone on energy-driven Eurozone inflation could squeeze EUR/USD back toward $1.16-$1.17. DAX at 23,447 is the primary index play.

opportunities.company_name: US-Iran War / Strait of Hormuz Closure
opportunities.event_name: US-Iran Conflict — Strait of Hormuz Fully Closed
opportunities.recommended_action: partial de-risk; monitor for ceasefire or escalation
opportunities.rationale: Iran has effectively shut the Strait of Hormuz, choking ~20% of global oil supply. WTI rose 3.11% to $98.71, Goldman Sachs raised Brent target to $110 for March. JPMorgan warned sustained oil above $90 could trigger a 10-15% S&P 500 correction with domino effects on global growth. Any credible de-escalation headline will produce sharp relief across risk assets; renewed escalation will send WTI toward $105+ and accelerate the equity sell-off. Maintain partial hedge via long USOIL / short DE40 until ceasefire is confirmed.

opportunities.event_name: US Initial & Continuing Jobless Claims — week ending Mar 14
opportunities.rationale: Following a soft February payrolls print of 92K, weekly claims on 19 Mar will be the first post-FOMC labour market read. In a stagflation narrative, rising claims (>240K) would paradoxically reinforce dovish FOMC pricing but also deepen recession fears. Falling claims (<210K) would keep the labour market tight and validate Powell's cautious tone. Best traded as a secondary overlay to post-FOMC positioning rather than standalone.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter long on intraday dip into 5050-5120 where RSI resets below 55 on the 1H chart; stop below 4940 daily close. Add on a confirmed FOMC-driven dip.
opportunities.rationale: Gold is in a verified uptrend, supported by three converging tailwinds: Iran war safe-haven demand, stagflation-driven real rate compression, and institutional accumulation as BTC ETF inflows spill over into broader safe-haven assets. Current price at $5,183 is elevated; entry on a controlled pullback into the 5050-5120 zone offers a 1:2.6 risk-reward toward the 5480 prior swing high. Stop below 4940 respects the 5-day low.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Buy dip to 95-97 on any temporary de-escalation noise that fails to breach 91.50. Exit entire position immediately on confirmed ceasefire or OPEC+ emergency supply release above 2Mbpd.
opportunities.rationale: WTI surged 3.11% to $98.71 as Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz. Goldman's $110 Brent target implies a further 10-12% upside on WTI. A dip to the 95-97 zone (retesting the breakout of the $90 resistance area) offers a defined re-entry. Thesis invalidated by a durable ceasefire or coordinated SPR release by IEA members, so stops must be respected and sizing conservative.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter short on any intraday bounce toward 1.1380-1.1440 before FOMC. Re-assess if ECB delivers hawkish surprise on 19 Mar that pushes EUR/USD back above 1.1550.
opportunities.rationale: EUR/USD slipped 0.77% to $1.14 on risk-off USD flows driven by the Iran conflict and stagflation fears. With FOMC expected to hold rates and ECB under pressure from soft European growth, the USD has a dual safe-haven and rate-differential tailwind. The 1.10-1.11 area is the next key support; the 1.1550 level is clear invalidation (prior structure resistance). Trade FOMC direction dependent — a hawkish Fed accelerates the move.

opportunities.trade_setup: dead-cat bounce short into event cluster
opportunities.entry_trigger: Short any bounce into 23500-23700 resistance before ECB (19 Mar). Exit fully on confirmed Iran de-escalation or dovish ECB surprise that pushes DAX above 24100.
opportunities.rationale: DAX at 23,447 is down 0.60% on the day and remains highly exposed to two intersecting risks: energy costs via Iran (Germany is heavily oil-import dependent) and ECB policy via Lagarde on 19 Mar. Any bounce into the 23,500-23,700 zone (prior breakdown level) offers a short entry with a defined stop above 24,100. Target is the 22,200 area which aligns with the YTD low cluster.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter long only AFTER FOMC statement (18 Mar 20:00 CET) if Powell does NOT deliver hawkish dot-plot surprise and NAS100 holds above 23,100. Skip entirely if PPI prints hot or Powell signals fewer than 1 cut in 2026.
opportunities.rationale: NAS100 at ~23,956 has been under sustained pressure from rate uncertainty and AI spend scrutiny. In a benign FOMC scenario (hold + neutral guidance), beaten-down mega-cap tech (NVDA $180, MSFT $395, AMZN $207, GOOG $301) could see a brief positioning unwind relief rally toward 24,900. Conviction is LOW because macro headwinds (Iran, stagflation) are still firmly in play; this is a tactical post-event trade only, not a trend change.
